div.h2: Entry/Exit Toggle Correctness Across Interleaved PIDs
div.p: Per-PID state machines prevent toggle corruption from event interleaving

g.text: ✅ CORRECT: Per-PID State Machines (pid_state_t in hash map)
❌ BROKEN: Single Global in_syscall Toggle (M1 naive approach)

div.p: Key Invariant: pid_map_get(&g_pid_map, pid) dispatches each event to the correct pid_state_t. PID 1235's events never touch PID 1234's in_syscall toggle and vice versa.

Corruption: Global toggle treats every waitpid(-1) return as belonging to the same single process. After Event 2 (PID 1235 entry), the global toggle flips to 0, so Event 3 (PID 1234 exit) is misread as an entry. orig_rax is read instead of rax — producing the syscall number (1=write) as the "return value". All subsequent lines are garbage.

text.tspan: ⚠️ rax read as EXIT — which syscall?!
